stdout:
**Acceptance Criteria**
No blocking findings. `WorktreeConfig.mode` and `enforce_leases` are now typed as `Option<WorktreeMode>` / `Option<EnforcementMode>` with shared `FromStr` + serde deserialization in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:867). Env-var parsing now also goes through the same `FromStr` path in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:1406).

`ItemKindConfig.scope` is typed as `Option<KindScopeConfig>` and invalid tokens fail during TOML deserialization in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:319). The domain conversion now handles only typed variants in [domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:482).

**Architecture**
No new I/O or storage access was introduced outside storage. The change is limited to config parsing, command diagnostics, and domain conversion of already-loaded config values. The existing domain-to-config coupling in `ItemKind::from_config` remains, but this change does not materially expand the architectural boundary beyond the requested typed config handoff.

**Functional Paradigm**
The changed code uses expression-oriented parsing and direct typed matches. The required dispatch-table collapse is implemented through `FromStr` rather than duplicated string matches.

**Correctness And Invariants**
No file-write, event-log, gate-transition, assignee/label, or PID code paths were changed. Gate semantics and PID safety are unaffected by this diff.

**Safety**
No unsafe code was introduced. The workspace-level `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` requirement is not weakened.

**Testing**
The issue context reports `cargo-ci` passed with exit code `0` at `2026-06-25T23:16:29Z`; per instruction I did not rerun cargo tests. New unit tests cover invalid scope parse-time failure and worktree/enforcement case handling in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:2248).

**Error Handling**
New parser errors use `thiserror` and include the invalid token, e.g. [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:834) and [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:857). Fallible env parsing still returns `Result` with context in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/config.rs:1408).

**CLI Contract**
No command surface or `--json` handling was removed. CLI config validation now uses the same typed parsers in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-70ca9e3a/crates/jit/src/main.rs:3960).

**Documentation**
The new public enums and parser error types have doc comments and examples, including `KindScopeConfig`, `WorktreeMode`, and `EnforcementMode`.

**Jit Issue Management**
`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there is no prerequisite work to verify. I ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with `✓ Repository validation passed`. Prior review history is empty, so there are no unresolved prior-review items.

VERDICT: PASS
